title: Pungent Mustardy Thai Tofu Kanom Ko
description: A mustardy, shaggy, lazy-sunday Thai dish featuring red wine vinegar and toast, made with ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly ingredients in the form of kanom-ko.

ingredients.name: firm tofu
ingredients.notes: drained and cut into small cubes

instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Toast the bread slices until they are lightly browned.
In a blender or food processor, combine the mustard paste, red wine vinegar, salt, and black pepper, and blend until smooth.
In a large pan, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat.
Add the cubed tofu to the pan and cook until it is lightly browned on all sides.
Add the blended mustard mixture to the pan and stir to combine with the tofu.
Transfer the tofu mixture to a baking dish and top with toasted bread slices and chopped scallions.
Bake the kanom ko in the preheated oven for 15 minutes, or until the bread is crispy and the tofu is heated through.
